edges.description: Ectopic germ-cell-derived tumor cells retain multilineage differentiation potential.
Differentiated teratomatous tissue forms a space-occupying CNS mass.
Nongerminomatous CNS germ cell tumor components can present with endocrine manifestations including precocious puberty.
Copy-number altered teratomatous components can contribute to growth and malignant transformation.
edges.source: CNS Germ Cell Tumor Copy-Number Alteration

edges.description: Raised intracranial pressure and CNS mass effect commonly present with headache.
Pineal or ventricular mass effect can obstruct cerebrospinal-fluid pathways.
Tumor mass effect in midline CNS structures can affect visual pathways.
Pineal-region mass effect can involve the dorsal midbrain and produce Parinaud sign.
Raised intracranial pressure and hydrocephalus can produce vomiting.
intermediate_mechanisms: Raised intracranial pressure or obstructive hydrocephalus.

edges.description: Raised intracranial pressure and tumor-related vomiting can produce nausea.
intermediate_mechanisms: Raised intracranial pressure or tumor-related vomiting.

edges.description: Suprasellar or hypothalamic-pituitary mass effect can produce diabetes insipidus.
Enlarging teratomatous tissue can worsen compressive CNS manifestations despite marker normalization.
Continued enlargement despite normalized tumor markers creates a rationale for surgical resection.
intermediate_mechanisms: Persistent tumor enlargement despite normalized markers.
edges.target: Second-Look Surgery for Growing Teratoma Syndrome

edges.source: Endoscopic Third Ventriculostomy or CSF Diversion

edges.target: CNS Germ Cell Tumor Copy-Number Alteration

nodes.description: CNS teratomas and related CNS germ cell tumors can show copy-number alterations. A recent malignant-feature growing teratoma syndrome case identified DMRT1 loss and 12p gain, supporting a copy-number alteration mechanism in at least some aggressive teratomatous lesions.
nodes.id: CNS Germ Cell Tumor Copy-Number Alteration

evidence.reference_title: Successful Multimodal Treatment of Intracranial Growing Teratoma Syndrome with Malignant Features.
evidence.snippet: Methylation classifier analysis confirmed the diagnosis of teratoma, and DMRT1 loss and 12p gain were identified by copy number variation analysis, potentially elucidating the cause of growth and malignant transformation of the teratoma.

meta.association: Somatic copy-number alteration in a malignant-feature iGTS case

evidence.reference_title: Successful Multimodal Treatment of Intracranial Growing Teratoma Syndrome with Malignant Features.
evidence.snippet: Methylation classifier analysis confirmed the diagnosis of teratoma, and DMRT1 loss and 12p gain were identified by copy number variation analysis, potentially elucidating the cause of growth and malignant transformation of the teratoma.

nodes.description: Diabetes insipidus can occur with suprasellar or hypothalamic-pituitary axis involvement by CNS germ cell tumors.

evidence.reference_title: Primary central nervous system germ cell tumors in Central America and the Caribbean Region: an AHOPCA 20-year experience.
evidence.snippet: Presenting symptoms were headaches (n = 24, 50%), visual disturbances (n = 17, 35.4%), vomiting (n = 12, 25%), nausea (n = 8, 16.7%), and diabetes insipidus (n = 7, 14.6%).

nodes.description: CSF diversion can be needed before tumor resection when a pineal or ventricular CNS teratoma causes obstructive hydrocephalus.
nodes.id: Endoscopic Third Ventriculostomy or CSF Diversion

evidence.reference_title: Pediatric pineal region tumors: institutional experience of surgical managements with posterior interhemispheric transtentorial approach.
evidence.snippet: It was treated prior to the craniotomy for tumor resection with endoscopic third ventriculostomy (ETV) in 33, external ventricular drainage in 26, and precraniotomy shunt in 15.

nodes.description: Growth of teratomatous tissue within restricted CNS spaces compresses neural structures and obstructs cerebrospinal fluid pathways, especially in pineal, suprasellar, ventricular, and spinal locations.

evidence.reference_title: Pediatric pineal region tumors: institutional experience of surgical managements with posterior interhemispheric transtentorial approach.
evidence.snippet: Hydrocephalus caused the main presenting symptoms and was noted in 74 patients.

evidence.reference_title: Primary central nervous system germ cell tumors in Central America and the Caribbean Region: an AHOPCA 20-year experience.
evidence.snippet: Presenting symptoms were headaches (n = 24, 50%), visual disturbances (n = 17, 35.4%), vomiting (n = 12, 25%), nausea (n = 8, 16.7%), and diabetes insipidus (n = 7, 14.6%).

nodes.description: During treatment of CNS germ cell tumor, mature or immature teratomatous components may continue to enlarge despite normalization of AFP or beta-hCG, requiring surgical management.

evidence.reference_title: Intracranial growing teratoma syndrome (iGTS): an international case series and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: PURPOSE: Intracranial growing teratoma syndrome (iGTS) is a rare phenomenon of paradoxical growth of a germ cell tumor (GCT) during treatment despite normalization of tumor markers.

nodes.description: Headache is a common presenting symptom in pediatric CNS germ cell tumor cohorts and likely reflects mass effect or raised intracranial pressure.

evidence.reference_title: Primary central nervous system germ cell tumors in Central America and the Caribbean Region: an AHOPCA 20-year experience.
evidence.snippet: Presenting symptoms were headaches (n = 24, 50%), visual disturbances (n = 17, 35.4%), vomiting (n = 12, 25%), nausea (n = 8, 16.7%), and diabetes insipidus (n = 7, 14.6%).

nodes.description: Pineal-region teratomas can obstruct cerebrospinal fluid flow and produce hydrocephalus.

evidence.reference_title: Pediatric pineal region tumors: institutional experience of surgical managements with posterior interhemispheric transtentorial approach.
evidence.snippet: Hydrocephalus caused the main presenting symptoms and was noted in 74 patients.

nodes.description: Teratomatous tumor cells differentiate into tissue derivatives from ectodermal, mesodermal, and endodermal lineages, producing mature, immature, mixed, or transformed histologic patterns.

evidence.reference_title: Management of central nervous system teratoma.
evidence.snippet: Central nervous system (CNS) teratomas are very rare neoplasms that contain tissues derived from all three germ cell layers (endoderm, mesoderm, and ectoderm).

evidence.reference_title: Teratomas in central nervous system: a clinico-morphological study with review of literature.
evidence.snippet: Morphologically, a variety of tissue derivatives were seen in the cases.

nodes.description: Nausea may accompany raised intracranial pressure or tumor-related vomiting.

evidence.reference_title: Primary central nervous system germ cell tumors in Central America and the Caribbean Region: an AHOPCA 20-year experience.
evidence.snippet: Presenting symptoms were headaches (n = 24, 50%), visual disturbances (n = 17, 35.4%), vomiting (n = 12, 25%), nausea (n = 8, 16.7%), and diabetes insipidus (n = 7, 14.6%).

nodes.description: Parinaud syndrome or Parinaud sign can occur with pineal-region tumors affecting the dorsal midbrain, including teratomas within pineal-region germ cell tumor cohorts.

evidence.reference_title: Pediatric pineal region tumors: institutional experience of surgical managements with posterior interhemispheric transtentorial approach.
evidence.snippet: Parinaud's sign was noted in 24 patients, of which 16 were transient.

nodes.description: Precocious puberty is an uncommon presentation in nongerminomatous CNS germ cell tumors and may reflect beta-hCG secretion in some tumor components.

evidence.reference_title: Primary central nervous system germ cell tumors in Central America and the Caribbean Region: an AHOPCA 20-year experience.
evidence.snippet: Two patients with NGGCT presented with precocious puberty.

nodes.description: CNS teratomas arise within the CNS germ cell tumor spectrum, where the prevailing developmental model is that primordial germ cells or germ-cell-like precursors become ectopically located in midline CNS structures and then form tumors with embryonic differentiation potential.

evidence.reference_title: The Japan Society for Neuro-Oncology guideline on the diagnosis and treatment of central nervous system germ cell tumors.
evidence.snippet: In line with the hypothesis that the primordial germ cell is the cell-of-origin, histopathological examinations for this pathology involve a diverse range of components mirroring the embryogenic developmental dimensions.

nodes.description: Surgical resection is the key treatment for intracranial growing teratoma syndrome or residual enlarging teratomatous mass after treatment.
nodes.id: Second-Look Surgery for Growing Teratoma Syndrome

evidence.reference_title: Intracranial growing teratoma syndrome (iGTS): an international case series and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: All patients underwent surgical resection, leading to gross total resection in 79%.

evidence.reference_title: Intracranial growing teratoma syndrome (iGTS): an international case series and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: Complete surgical resection is the mainstay of treatment.

nodes.description: Visual symptoms may arise from pineal, suprasellar, intracranial pressure, or other midline CNS involvement.

evidence.reference_title: Primary central nervous system germ cell tumors in Central America and the Caribbean Region: an AHOPCA 20-year experience.
evidence.snippet: Presenting symptoms were headaches (n = 24, 50%), visual disturbances (n = 17, 35.4%), vomiting (n = 12, 25%), nausea (n = 8, 16.7%), and diabetes insipidus (n = 7, 14.6%).

nodes.description: Vomiting can accompany raised intracranial pressure, obstructive hydrocephalus, or mass effect.

evidence.reference_title: Primary central nervous system germ cell tumors in Central America and the Caribbean Region: an AHOPCA 20-year experience.
evidence.snippet: Presenting symptoms were headaches (n = 24, 50%), visual disturbances (n = 17, 35.4%), vomiting (n = 12, 25%), nausea (n = 8, 16.7%), and diabetes insipidus (n = 7, 14.6%).
